L1.area: OAuth-based authentication and identity provider integration UI

L2.description: A cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists in the OAuth login flow where user-controlled parameters sent via a GET request are reflected into the rendered HTML page without proper output escaping. This allows an attacker to inject arbitrary JavaScript and wiki syntax from the login screen, enabling remote code execution via script macros. The vulnerability affects the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of the entire installation.

L3.area: OAuth-based authentication and identity provider integration UI
L3.description: A cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists in the OAuth login flow where user-controlled parameters sent via a GET request are reflected into the rendered HTML page without proper output escaping. This allows an attacker to inject arbitrary JavaScript and wiki syntax from the login screen, enabling remote code execution via script macros. The vulnerability affects the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of the entire installation.

ground_truth.reason: User-controlled OAuth GET request parameters are reflected into HTML output without escaping, allowing XSS and XWiki syntax injection that enables remote code execution via Groovy macros.
